reasoning_model_1: The model failed significantly in Content Accuracy. Instead of reading the provided sentence once as requested, the audio contains the target sentence repeated three consecutive times ('我早上通常会先洗漱...不然要迟到了！' x3). This implies a failure in stop-token generation or a loop hallucination. Because the content is severely flawed and deviates from the script instruction (reading it once), it is impossible to fairly evaluate the dynamic speed adjustment within a single natural utterance. The repetitive nature makes the audio unusable and unnatural.
reasoning_model_2: The model followed the Content Accuracy requirements perfectly, speaking the text exactly as written without repetition or omission. Regarding the Paralinguistic Compliance (Dynamic Adjustment), the audio effectively demonstrates the requested change. It begins at a steady, medium pace during the first segment ('我早上通常会先洗漱，然后吃个简单的早餐'), establishing a baseline. As the sentence progresses to '看看新闻', the pace begins to tighten, and it culminates in a very fast, urgent delivery for the final segment ('接着就得赶紧出门上班了，不然要迟到了！'). The transition is smooth and conveys the intended sense of urgency effectively.
model_comparison: Model 1 exhibits a critical failure in generation mechanics by repeating the entire script three times. This violates the basic instruction and results in a very low score regardless of voice quality. Model 2, in contrast, produces the correct text exactly once and successfully executes the paralinguistic instruction to accelerate the speaking rate from medium to extremely fast. Model 2 is the clear winner as it produced a usable, compliant, and accurate audio file, whereas Model 1 produced a hallucinated loop.
